stories.headline: COOPER IS STAR; His Single Sends Home Deciding Tally for the Giants in Fifth MIZE AND RIGNEY CONNECT Homers Account for 3 Runs Against Brooks -- Victory on Mound to Voiselle GIANTS CONQUER DODGERS BY 4-3 DODGER NEWCOMER SHOWS SPEED ON THE BASEPATHS TO GET SINGLE
stories.summary: The Giants defeated the Dodgers 4-3, with home runs from Mize and Rigney, as the team's hitting streak continued during the post-war baseball season. Cooper's single scored the deciding run.

stories.headline: 'MORALE WRECKING' OF PHONE UNIONISTS LAID TO U. S. CHIEFS; Counsel Says Schwellenbach Had Stated That NFTW Was 'Weak' and Near 'Collapse' MINERS DONATE $100,000 Labor Department Will Hold 'Intensive Conferences' With View to Quick Settlement U. S. CHIEFS SCORED BY PHONE UNIONIST $100,000 FROM UMW FOR PHONE UNION
stories.summary: The Labor Department, led by Secretary Schwellenbach, is criticized by phone unionists for trying to break their strike, while the United Mine Workers of America donates $100,000 to support the phone union's efforts. The strike comes as the US government, under President Harry S. Truman, is trying to manage labor disputes.

stories.headline: BASIC, WIDER CUTS IN PRICES SOUGHT TO FIGHT INFLATION; ' Newburyport Plan' Held Good So Far as It Goes but More Are Urged to Adopt It ITS EFFECTS ARE STUDIED Senator Flanders Assays It as La Guardia Calls Praise for It 'Childish, Stupid' More Drastic Price-Cutting Urged In the Fight to Ward Off Inflation
stories.summary: Senator Flanders and others are urging businesses to adopt price-cutting measures, like the 'Newburyport Plan', to combat inflation in the post-war economy. La Guardia calls for more drastic action to prevent inflation.

stories.headline: HOUSE GROUP VOTES BILL TO END STRIKES BY COURT INJUNCTION; Measure Would Empower U.S. to Halt Such Tie-Ups as That Threatened by Phone Union EARLY PASSAGE IS PRESSED A.T.&T. Long Lines Here Lays In Food, Cots, Anticipating Walkout Monday at 6 A. M. LINE IS BUSY AS TELEPHONE OPERATORS VOTE ON STRIKE HOUSE GROUP VOTES ANTI-STRIKE BILL
stories.summary: A House group votes to approve a bill that would allow the government to end strikes, like the one threatened by the phone union, through court injunction, as President Harry S. Truman's administration tries to manage labor disputes. The bill aims to prevent work stoppages that could disrupt essential services.

rounds.blurb: The Giants defeated the Dodgers 4-3, with home runs from Mize and Rigney. Labor disputes escalated as the phone union strike continued, with the Labor Department facing criticism. The government sought to manage inflation and strikes through legislation and price-cutting measures.

stories.headline: THRILLING STORY BY TITANIC'S SURVIVING WIRELESS MAN; Bride Tells How He and Phillips Worked and How He Finished a Stoker Who Tried to Steal Phillips's Life Belt -- Ship Sank to Tune of "Autumn"
stories.summary: The Titanic sank while its band played a song, and survivors described acts of heroism and tragedy, including many women refusing to be rescued without their husbands, as the ship's wireless operators, including H Bride and Phillips, sent out distress calls

stories.headline: WEST VIRGINIA ALSO WON BY ROOSEVELT; Colonel Carries 24 Counties, Which Gives Him a Majority of State Convention Delegates.
stories.summary: Theodore Roosevelt won a primary in West Virginia, carrying 24 counties and gaining a majority of state convention delegates

stories.headline: SAIONJI'S REPLY ENDS MAGDALENA BUGABOO; Japanese Premier's Message to The Time, Washington Agrees, Closes the "Incident."
stories.summary: Japanese Premier Saionji's message ended the Magdalena incident, with Washington considering the matter closed and President Taft preparing a reply to a resolution by Lodge

stories.headline: $100,000,000 PRICE ON RAILWAY MERGER; New Haven Must Spend This on Improvements, Says Foss, to Take Over Boston & Maine.
stories.summary: The New Haven railway company must spend $100 million on improvements to take over the Boston & Maine railway, according to Governor Foss

rounds.blurb: The Titanic sank while its band played, resulting in tragic losses and heroic acts. The ship's wireless operators sent out distress calls as it went down. Theodore Roosevelt won a primary in West Virginia during this time.

stories.headline: THE GREAT CONTEST.; Gathering of the Forces for the Struggle. Wonderful Unanimity Throughout the North and West. MOVEMENTS OF THE VOLUNTEERS. The Seventh Regiment of New-York Going to Washington. MOVEMENTS AT MONTGOMERY. Letters of Marque and Reprisal to be Issued. MORE VOLUNTEERS CALLED FOR. The Virginia Convention Not yet Decided. THE PRESIDENT AND GEN. SCOTT. THE FORT SUMTER EXPEDITION. THE FIDELITY OF MAJOR ANDERSON. RUMORS FROM VIRGINIA. THE FORCES AT THE CAPITAL. PUBLIC SPIRIT IN WASHINGTON. LIEUT. SLEMMER AND FORT PICKENS. CAPT. HUGER'S RESIGNATION. STRICKEN FROM THE ROLL. THE DEFENCE OF HARPER'S FERRY. ENLISTMENTS IN BALTIMORE. THE POSITION OF JOHN M. BOTTS. A TELEGRAPHIC ERROR. DISPATCH TO THE ASSOCIATED PRESS.
stories.summary: As the country prepares for war, Northern and Western states show unity and volunteers gather to head to Washington, with President Abraham Lincoln and General Scott making key decisions, and Major Anderson's fidelity is noted. The defense of Harper's Ferry and Fort Pickens is also a concern.

stories.headline: OUR SOUTHERN CORRESPONDENCE.; Pen-Photographs of Charleston and the Charlestonians. ANXIETY RELATIVE TO FORT SUMPTER. CONDITION OF AFFAIRS IN ALABAMA. Particulars of the Seizure of the Sppply Sloop Isabella.--TRADE AND AGRICULTURAL PROSPECTS. A NAUTICAL SCHOOL AT CHARLESTON--EXPECTED REVENUE DIFFICULTIE--NEW PATENT MEDICINE DODGES, ETC. THE TRIBUNE'S DISPATCHES FROM MONTGOMERY-- COMPLETION OF THE BATTERIES--RECKLESS WASTE OF PROVISIONS--THE CONVENTION, ETC.
stories.summary: In Charleston, there is anxiety about Fort Sumter, and the condition of affairs in Alabama is reported, with details on trade, agriculture, and the completion of batteries. The Tribune's dispatches from Montgomery provide insight into the situation.

stories.headline: A FLOOD OF TROOPS IN WASHINGTON; FOUR NEW-YORK REGIMENTS THERE. Concentration of Secessionist Troops at Norfolk. Neither Jeff. Davis nor General Beauregard in Virginia.--ALEX. H. STEPHENS AT RICHMOND.--The Avalanche of Men and Money from the North.--Princely Subscriptions from New-Yorkers.--Vigorous Military Preparationsat Albany.--THIRTY-NINE REGIMENTS CALLED FOR. NEW YORK TROOPS IN WASHINGTON. A FLOOD OF TROOPS IN WASHINGTON. IMPORTANT FROM WESTERN VIRGINIA.
stories.summary: Troops from New York regiments arrive in Washington, while secessionist troops concentrate at Norfolk, and Alexander H. Stephens is in Richmond. The North is sending a flood of men and money, with vigorous military preparations underway.

stories.headline: ENLISTMEN IS FOR THE WAR.; Movements of Volunteer Troops in and Through this City The Boston Quota en Route The Seventh Regiment to go To-Day Probable Arrival of the Rhode Island Troops this Morning Spontaneous Offering of Men and Money. MASSACHUSETTS TROOPS EN ROUTE FOR WASHINGTON. ARRIVAL IN THIS CITY THEIR RECEPTION AND DEPARTURE. A CARD. THE GREAT REBELLION. ANOTHER REGIMENT ARRIVED THE MASSACHUSETTS MILITARY IN JERSEY CITY. THE SEVENTH REGIMENT AND THE VETERAN CORPS OF THE NATIONAL GUARD. MARCHING ORDERS FOR THE SEVENTH REGIMENT. HEAD QUARTERS SEVENTH REGIMENT N.Y.S.M. FIRST REGIMENT NATIONAL GUARDS. THE TWELFTH REGIMENT. DRILL-ROOMS FOR THE SOLDIERY STILL ANOTHER DRILL-ROOM. THE UNION VOLUNTEERS. THE UNION GUARD VOLUNTEERS; THE BARRY MUSKETERES. THE CUBANS TO ARMS. FIREMEN AS CITY VOLUNTEERS. THE WARD SCHOOL BOYS PATRIOTIC. THE FREE ACADEMY BOYS FOR THE UNION. THE BONE AND SINEW AROUSED. COLUMBIA COLLEGE REBUKES THE TRAITORS. PROBABLE ARRIVAL OF THE RHOLE ISLAND TROOPS THIS MORNING. A VOLUNTEER ARTILLERY CORPS. NINTH REGIMENT.
stories.summary: Volunteer troops move through the city, with the Boston quota en route and the Seventh Regiment preparing to leave, as spontaneous offerings of men and money pour in, and Massachusetts troops head to Washington, with the Rhode Island troops expected to arrive soon.

rounds.blurb: The Fort Sumter expedition was underway as Northern and Western states unified and volunteers gathered to head to Washington. President Abraham Lincoln and General Scott made key decisions, while Major Anderson's fidelity was noted. The defense of Harper's Ferry and Fort Pickens was also a concern.
